title: PTSD Checklist for DSM-5 (PCL-5)
instruction: Below is a list of problems that people sometimes have in response to a very stressful experience. Keeping your worst event in mind, please read each problem carefully and then select a number to indicate how much you have been bothered by that problem in the past month. Respond with 20 numbers in sequence (score for item 1, then item 2, ... up to item 20).

questions.text: 1. Repeated, disturbing, and unwanted memories of the stressful experience?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 2. Repeated, disturbing dreams of the stressful experience?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 3. Suddenly feeling or acting as if the stressful experience were actually happening again (as if you were actually back there reliving it)?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 4. Feeling very upset when something reminded you of the stressful experience?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 5. Having strong physical reactions when something reminded you of the stressful experience (for example, heart pounding, trouble breathing, sweating)?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 6. Avoiding memories, thoughts, or feelings related to the stressful experience?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 7. Avoiding external reminders of the stressful experience (for example, people, places, conversations, activities, objects, or situations)?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 8. Trouble remembering important parts of the stressful experience?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 9. Having strong negative beliefs about yourself, other people, or the world (for example, having thoughts such as: I am bad, there is something seriously wrong with me, no one can be trusted, the world is completely dangerous)?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 10. Blaming yourself or someone else for the stressful experience or what happened after it?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 11. Having strong negative feelings such as fear, horror, anger, guilt, or shame?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 12. Loss of interest in activities that you used to enjoy?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 13. Feeling distant or cut off from other people?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 14. Trouble experiencing positive feelings (for example, being unable to feel happiness or have loving feelings for people close to you)?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 15. Irritable behavior, angry outbursts, or acting aggressively?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 16. Taking too many risks or doing things that could cause you harm?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 17. Being "superalert" or watchful or on guard?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 18. Feeling jumpy or easily startled?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 19. Having difficulty concentrating?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely

questions.text: 20. Trouble falling or staying asleep?
questions.options: 0=Not at all 1=A little bit 2=Moderately 3=Quite a bit 4=Extremely
